LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics_mini.label: basic-density hydroponics bay
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics_mini.description: An entry-tier dense hydroponics bay that fits 16 plants into a 1x4 footprint, yielding roughly four times the growing area of ordinary planting in the same space. It can rely on sunlight or switch on a built-in sun lamp at extra power cost, does not regulate temperature, and a power loss will kill the crop.
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics.label: high-density hydroponics bay
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics.description: An industrial dense hydroponics bay that supports 80 plants in a 2x4 footprint, delivering roughly ten times the growing area of ordinary planting in the same space. Its nutrient circulation keeps the stack productive, and its built-in sun lamp can trade extra power for light independence, but it still needs steady power and external temperature control.
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics_Hyper.label: ultra-density hydroponics bay
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics_Hyper.description: An advanced ultra-dense hydroponics bay built for large indoor farms, packing 240 plants into a 2x6 footprint for roughly twenty times the growing area of ordinary planting in the same space. The stacked grow layers are extremely productive, and its built-in sun lamp can trade extra power for light independence, but it still requires continuous power and careful climate management to avoid a total crop loss.
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics_Quantum.label: quantum-density hydroponics bay
LanguageData.HDH_Hydroponics_Quantum.description: A spacer-grade quantum hydroponics chamber that folds multiple grow layers into a compact 2x2 footprint. Its capacity scales upward with available power, and its built-in sun lamp can be toggled to trade additional energy use for light independence while ignoring ordinary temperature limits.
